Title: Metabolic pathways in tropical dicotyledonous albuminous seeds: Coffea arabica as a case study

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represents a strong candidate plasma membrane hexose transporter associated with early intracellular hexose uptake during seed development.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Maximal expression at early developmental stages when perisperm predominates.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Maximal expression at early developmental stages when perisperm predominates.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seeds across seven developmental stages under field conditions.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Real-time RT-PCR expression profiling correlated with metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among three putative hexose transporters, EE199490 showed maximal early-stage expression. 2. Its expression pattern matches the period of high glucose/fructose abundance in transient perisperm. 3. It was proposed as a candidate for functional characterization in early hexose uptake.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted transcript profiling by real-time RT-PCR of 137 genes; metabolite profiling; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a candidate transporter; no direct transport assay reported.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modify early seed carbon import and reserve partitioning, but function requires direct validation.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering early sugar uptake could affect cell division and overall seed development.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides carbon and energy for developing seed tissues.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhanced sucrose transporter expression is strongly associated with increased sucrose accumulation during mid-stages of endosperm development.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Both identified transporter genes showed maximal expression at stages 3–4.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Both identified transporter genes showed maximal expression at stages 3–4 during rapid endosperm development.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seeds across seven developmental stages under field conditions.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Real-time RT-PCR expression profiling correlated with sucrose content profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Two sucrose/H+ cotransporter genes were identified. 2. Both peaked at stages 3–4 when endosperm rapidly developed and storage began. 3. Their expression was interpreted as transcriptional control of sucrose import during storage.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted transcript profiling by real-time RT-PCR of 137 genes; metabolite profiling; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Genes were not individually named in the text; inference is based on coordinated expression rather than direct transporter assays.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential targets to increase sucrose accumulation in coffee seed.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changing sucrose import may alter carbon allocation among lipids, polysaccharides and phenolics.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reserve carbohydrate and readily mobilized energy source during germination.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves sucrose with UDP to produce UDP-glucose and fructose.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to sucrose catabolism associated with increased sucrose import and storage-phase carbon utilization.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: sus1 was closely associated with sucrose transporter expression and most active during stages 3–4.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest during developing endosperm storage phase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Real-time RT-PCR expression profiling correlated with metabolite changes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. sus1 expression was closely associated with sucrose transporter expression. 2. It is likely involved in sucrose catabolism during endosperm storage. 3. It contrasts with SPS/SPP, which peak earlier in perisperm.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development stages under field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional role inferred from expression association; no enzyme assay in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to redirect imported sucrose into reserve biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may affect broad carbon partitioning.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts mannose-1-phosphate to GDP-mannose for mannan biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High transcript accumulation at onset of CWP deposition supports a key role in providing activated mannose for endosperm galactomannan synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcription peak concomitant with onset of CWP accumulation between stages 3 and 6.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcription peak at onset of endosperm cell wall thickening.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Real-time RT-PCR profiling with metabolite/cell wall accumulation correlation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MGT is one of the key genes peaking with onset of CWP accumulation. 2. It marks hemicellulosic polysaccharide biosynthesis in coffee endosperm. 3. It suggests transcriptional control of galactomannan formation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to alter endosperm wall polysaccharide quantity and composition.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May modify seed texture and processing quality.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major structural/storage polysaccharide of coffee endosperm

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: glycosyltransferase involved in mannan backbone synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes mannose residues to form the β-1,4-mannan backbone of galactomannan.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript peak coincides with onset of CWP accumulation, indicating direct involvement in galactomannan deposition.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Peaks with onset of CWP accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed during rapid endosperm development and wall thickening.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Real-time RT-PCR profiling with metabolite/cell wall accumulation correlation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MS expression coincides with rapid CWP accumulation. 2. It is a signature gene for hemicellulosic polysaccharide biosynthesis. 3. It likely directly drives mannan backbone deposition in endosperm walls.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering coffee bean wall polysaccharide content and texture.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changes could affect seed hardness and roasting/extraction properties.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactose residues to the mannan backbone to generate galactomannan branching.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Peaks with CWP accumulation and is implicated in galactose-to-mannan branching determining galactomannan structure.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Peak expression at onset/mid phase of CWP accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Concomitant with rapid endosperm wall thickening; also discussed around 150 DAF with declining degree of galactomannan substitution.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Real-time RT-PCR profiling with developmental metabolite and histological comparison
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GMGT peaks during CWP accumulation. 2. It is linked to galactomannan branching structure. 3. Its expression matches developmental changes in galactomannan substitution.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for modifying galactomannan branching and bean quality traits.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter wall architecture and processing properties.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: UDP-glucuronic acid supply for hemicellulose/cell wall synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes myo-inositol to glucuronic acid, feeding cell wall precursor biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strong transcription peak at stages 4–5 supports a functional myo-inositol oxidation pathway during endosperm wall thickening.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Peak coincides with cell wall thickening in developing endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Real-time RT-PCR profiling with metabolite correlation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MIOX peaks exactly when endosperm wall thickening occurs. 2. It supports glucuronic acid production from myo-inositol. 3. The study provides evidence for a functional myo-inositol oxidation pathway in coffee seed CWP biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate precursor supply for cell wall polysaccharides.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May impact phytic acid, inositol metabolism, and wall composition.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Fatty acid biosynthesis leading to triacylglycerol accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes palmitoyl-ACP to release 16:0 from plastid fatty acid synthesis into the acyl-CoA pool.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as key control point for unusual high palmitate accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High FATb transcription during lipid synthesis is proposed to underlie the unusual high palmitic acid content of coffee endosperm oil.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Pronounced coordinated increase during stage with highest lipid synthesis rate.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High during endosperm lipid synthesis, coordinated with KASII, SAD, FATA and Δ12 desaturase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Real-time RT-PCR profiling with fatty acid composition analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FATb is highly transcribed during endosperm lipid synthesis. 2. Its expression pattern is proposed to explain the unusually high palmitic acid content of coffee oil. 3. The paper highlights FATb as a major determinant of the balanced 16:0:18:2 composition.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted real-time RT-PCR; lipid and fatty acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for modifying coffee oil fatty acid composition.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changing FATb may alter membrane lipid balance and total oil properties.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Storage lipid composition affecting seed quality

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Diacylglycerol and phosphocholine donor / phosphatidylcholine (reversible interconversion)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Phosphatidylcholine or diacylglycerol depending on direction
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes reversible interconversion between DAG and phosphatidylcholine, enabling flux of desaturated acyl groups from PC back to DAG.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as major route for endosperm TAG enrichment in linoleic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PC-DAG interconversion mediated by CPT appears to be the major route for TAG enrichment in linoleic acid in developing coffee endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Significant increase in transcription at mid-stages.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mid-stage expression increase coincident with TAG storage genes in developing endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Real-time RT-PCR profiling integrated with fatty acid composition and pathway interpretation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPT expression increases significantly at mid-stages. 2. Its timing matches TAG storage and linoleate enrichment. 3. The study concludes PC-DAG interconversion is the major route for endosperm TAG enrichment in 18:2.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted real-time RT-PCR; lipid/fatty acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key engineering target to alter PUFA enrichment of coffee oil.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulating CPT may affect phosphatidylcholine homeostasis and membrane composition.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a Δ12 double bond into oleate on phosphatidylcholine to generate linoleate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to production of 18:2 required for the high linoleic acid content of coffee seed TAG.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Exhibits maximal transcription at stages 4–5; coordinated rise during high lipid synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Peaks in developing endosperm concomitantly with TAG assembly/storage genes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Real-time RT-PCR profiling with fatty acid composition analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EE196983 peaks at stages 4–5. 2. It coincides with endosperm TAG storage and unsaturation processes. 3. Together with CPT, it supports the route enriching TAG in linoleic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted real-time RT-PCR; lipid/fatty acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to tune 18:2 content in coffee oil.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered unsaturation can affect membrane properties and oxidative stability.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes acyl-CoA-dependent acylation of DAG at the sn-3 position to form TAG.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant for TAG assembly in endosperm context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Endosperm-specific DAGAT transcript peaks at stages 4–5, indicating a key role in final TAG assembly during storage.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Maximal expression at mid-stages in endosperm; contrasted with perisperm paralog DV697030 at stage 1.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Peaks during endosperm TAG storage phase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Real-time RT-PCR profiling with lipid accumulation dynamics
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Two DAGAT paralogs showed tissue-specific expression programs. 2. DV711950 is assigned to endosperm lipid synthesis. 3. It peaks with final TAG assembly and oil body protein synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted real-time RT-PCR; lipid/fatty acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime target to increase storage oil accumulation in coffee endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher TAG synthesis may change carbon allocation away from sugars or cell wall compounds.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates phenylalanine to cinnamate at the entry of the phenylpropanoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High expression at stages 3–4 coincides with maximal CGA biosynthetic activity, supporting a role in CGA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Coincides with rapid endosperm development and CGA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Real-time RT-PCR profiling with CGA profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL1 is among upstream phenylpropanoid genes induced during the CGA accumulation window. 2. Its expression supports active precursor supply to CGA biosynthesis. 3. It is part of a coordinated transcriptional set during stages 3–4.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to alter total phenylpropanoid flux and CGA content.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could also affect lignin and defense phenolics.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant phenolic reserve of coffee seeds

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates cinnamate to p-coumarate in the core phenylpropanoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: suggested central position due to single-copy transcript and transient induction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Pronounced transient C4H transcript peak at stages 3–4 matches peak CGA storage, indicating a major role in coffee CGA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High expression at stages 3–4; pronounced transient peak.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Coincides with maximal CGA biosynthetic activity in developing seed.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: developing endosperm and earlier perisperm contribution
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Real-time RT-PCR profiling with CGA profiling and comparative interpretation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C4H is strongly induced at stages 3–4. 2. Its expression matches the short time window of intense CGA accumulation. 3. The authors highlight C4H as a major central gene in coffee phenylpropanoid flux to CGA.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate to modify CGA accumulation in coffee seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad effects on lignin and other phenylpropanoids likely.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major antioxidant and bioactive phenolic in coffee

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamates to CoA thioesters for downstream CGA and lignin metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Two 4CL genes are induced during stages 3–4 and likely contribute to CGA biosynthetic activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Real-time RT-PCR profiling with CGA profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AM117807 and EH662404 are among the genes whose expression coincides with maximal CGA accumulation. 2. They likely supply hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA precursors. 3. Different 4CL family members may contribute differentially to CGA versus other phenylpropanoid branches.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential targets to influence hydroxycinnamate flux and CGA content.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Might affect lignification and other phenylpropanoid-derived traits.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: caffeoyl quinate anabolism and catabolism / lignin rerouting
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA with quinate/shikimate; also 5-CQA in reverse/hydrolytic direction inferred
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups between CoA esters and quinate/shikimate; likely participates both in CGA synthesis and later CGA remobilization.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Bimodal HCT1 expression suggests a dual role in early caffeoyl quinate anabolism and late CGA catabolism toward lignin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Bimodal; one peak associated with CGA accumulation and another at late stages.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Late-stage high expression suggests role in CGA catabolism during endosperm hardening.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Real-time RT-PCR profiling; interpretation supported by prior recombinant enzyme evidence from C. canephora cited in discussion
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HCT1 shows a bimodal expression pattern. 2. Early expression suggests involvement in caffeoyl quinate anabolism. 3. Late expression supports a role in CGA remobilization toward lignin during endosperm hardening.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion cites recombinant C. canephora HCT hydrolyzing 5-CQA, supporting catabolic interpretation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target to alter both CGA accumulation and remobilization.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may strongly affect lignin formation and seed hardening.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: CGA accumulation and phenylpropanoid flux control
